title: Docker Registry
content:
### Docker Hub

Docker Hub is the default public registry. It hosts millions of images including official images from software vendors.

### docker login

Authenticate with a registry:

```bash
docker login                    # Docker Hub
docker login myregistry.com     # private registry
```

Credentials are stored in Docker's config file (`~/.docker/config.json`).

### docker tag

Tag an image for pushing to a registry:

```bash
docker tag my-app:latest username/my-app:latest
docker tag my-app:latest myregistry.com/my-app:v1
```

### docker push

Upload an image to a registry:

```bash
docker push username/my-app:latest
docker push myregistry.com/my-app:v1
```

### Private Registries

Run your own registry:

```bash
docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --name registry registry:2
docker tag my-app localhost:5000/my-app
docker push localhost:5000/my-app
```

Cloud providers offer managed registries:
- AWS ECR (Elastic Container Registry)
- Azure Container Registry (ACR)
- Google Container Registry (GCR) / Artifact Registry

### Docker Hub Repositories

- **Public repos**: Visible to everyone, free with unlimited pulls
- **Private repos**: Only accessible to authorized users (free tier includes 1 private repo)

### Registry Authentication

Docker supports several authentication methods:
- Username/password (HTTPS)
- Token-based authentication
- Credential helpers (OS keychain)

For automation (CI/CD), use personal access tokens instead of passwords:

```bash
echo $PAT | docker login -u username --password-stdin
```
codeExample:
docker tag nginx:latest myusername/nginx:custom
docker push myusername/nginx:custom
docker pull myusername/nginx:custom

quiz.question: Which command uploads a local image to a registry?
What is the purpose of `docker tag` before pushing?
options: It is required before every push
It adds a label for versioning
It renames the image with the registry path
